page: Amazon Web Services t2.large is a burstable performance instance belonging to the t2 family, utilizing the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, providing 8.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the t2.large sits in the lower performance tiers for intensive compute tasks. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the instance in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests also show weak to poor results, with uncached read and write speeds falling into the bottom 10%. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in text generation for small language models, despite weak prompt processing speeds.

Due to its burstable CPU allocation model, the t2.large offers a cost-effective solution for workloads that do not require continuous high CPU utilization. It is designed to accumulate CPU credits during idle periods to handle occasional performance spikes. This resource profile is best suited for low-traffic web serving, small database operations, basic development environments, and lightweight background tasks rather than sustained high-performance computing or large-scale data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services t2.large is a burstable general-purpose instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. Running at 2.3 GHz, it provides 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 8.0 GB of system memory, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on a 1 Gbps baseline network, its performance profile is characterized by burstable CPU allocation. Benchmark results place its compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations performance largely in the bottom 10% to 25%, though it achieves average speeds in text generation for small language models. Featuring a 45 MB L3 cache and AVX support, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is best suited for low-traffic web servers, development environments, and light database tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t2.large is a burstable x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 CPU, offering a cost-effective option for low-intensity, variable workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t2.large is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for low-intensity workloads.
tagline: A burstable virtual server with balanced memory and compute resources designed specifically for handling variable and low intensity workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor at 2.3 GHz
Configured with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
Burstable CPU allocation model for handling occasional spikes
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for low-traffic web serving and development environments
